solution: 
```java
class FoodRatings {
    private Map<String, TreeSet<Food>> cuisineToFoodMap;
    private Map<String, Food> foodMap;

    private class Food implements Comparable<Food> {
        String name;
        String cuisine;
        int rating;

        Food(String name, String cuisine, int rating) {
            this.name = name;
            this.cuisine = cuisine;
            this.rating = rating;
        }

        @Override
        public int compareTo(Food other) {
            if (this.rating != other.rating) {
                return Integer.compare(other.rating, this.rating); // higher rating first
            }
            return this.name.compareTo(other.name); // lexicographically smaller name first
        }
    }

    public FoodRatings(String[] foods, String[] cuisines, int[] ratings) {
        cuisineToFoodMap = new HashMap<>();
        foodMap = new HashMap<>();

        for (int i = 0; i < foods.length; i++) {
            String foodName = foods[i];
            String cuisine = cuisines[i];
            int rating = ratings[i];

            Food food = new Food(foodName, cuisine, rating);
            foodMap.put(foodName, food);

            cuisineToFoodMap.putIfAbsent(cuisine, new TreeSet<>());
            cuisineToFoodMap.get(cuisine).add(food);
        }
    }

    public void changeRating(String food, int newRating) {
        Food foodObj = foodMap.get(food);
        String cuisine = foodObj.cuisine;

        cuisineToFoodMap.get(cuisine).remove(foodObj);
        foodObj.rating = newRating;
        cuisineToFoodMap.get(cuisine).add(foodObj);
    }

    public String highestRated(String cuisine) {
        return cuisineToFoodMap.get(cuisine).first().name;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n) for changeRating and highestRated
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a TreeSet to maintain food items for each cuisine sorted by rating and lexicographical order. This allows efficient retrieval of the highest-rated food for a cuisine and updating ratings. The TreeSet ensures that operations like add, remove, and find are done in logarithmic time. The use of a HashMap for food lookup ensures constant time access to food items. The main weakness is the overhead of maintaining TreeSet, but it is justified by the efficient operations required.
